mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: CADASIL (Cerebral Autosomal Dominant Arteriopathy with Subcortical Infarcts and Leukoencephalopathy)
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: coma, language impairment, shuffling gait, internal carotid artery dissection, middle cerebral artery stenosis, perseverative thought
hypertension, vertigo, oromotor apraxia, renal involvement (hepatic steatosis could be secondary to kidney dysfunction), angiokeratomas (skin rash is often present but not mentioned here)
seizures (coma can be caused by prolonged seizure activity), developmental delay, speech problems
transient ischemic attacks (vertigo), cognitive decline (language impairment), moyamoya vessels formation (internal carotid artery dissection may be related)
vestibular schwannomas (can cause vertigo), cognitive deficits, scoliosis (shuffling gait), cafe-au-lait spots (not mentioned here but common feature)
epilepsy (may manifest as recurrent seizures leading to coma), intellectual disability, language difficulties, non-specific skin findings (facial angiofibromas, ash leaf macules)
short stature, elfin facies, cardiovascular anomalies (hypertension), hepatomegaly (similar presentation to hepatic steatosis), verbal overproduction (perseverative thoughts)

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Krabbe disease, atypical, due to saposin A deficiency
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: demyelination (possible causes of neurological symptoms including coma, language impairment, motor disturbances), jaundice (liver function abnormalities similar to hepatic steatosis)
tall stature, long limbs, pectus excavatum, ectopia lentis, dural ectasia (possibly causing intracranial hemorrhage resulting in coma)
choreiform movements (subtle signs might have been missed), dementia, psychiatric features (including perseveration)
